title: HE SHOU WU
description: He Shou Wu (Polygonum multiflorum) is a traditional Chinese medicinal herb primarily utilized for its potential anti-aging and restorative properties. It is thought to work by regulating key melanogenesis pathways and mitochondrial apoptosis, traditionally used to treat hair graying and age-related vitality loss.

research.title: Mechanistic Studies on the Use of Polygonum multiflorum for the Treatment of Hair Graying
research.summary: This study found that raw He Shou Wu promotes hair pigmentogenesis by increasing melanin content and regulating enzymes like tyrosinase, supporting its traditional use for blackening hair.

research.title: Mechanisms of Heshouwuyin in regulating apoptosis of testicular cells in aging rats through mitochondrial pathway
research.summary: Research suggests He Shou Wu-based formulas may protect against age-related cellular decline by regulating mitochondrial apoptosis pathways in reproductive tissues.

sentiment.summary: Users generally value the herb for hair and vitality benefits, but community consensus is cautious due to significant concerns regarding potential liver toxicity and the necessity of proper processing.

effects.label: Processing Matters:
effects.description: Raw roots are effective for hair but higher in laxative compounds; 'prepared' (cured with black bean) versions are preferred for tonic effects.

effects.label: Cumulative Results:
effects.description: Users note that benefits for hair color typically require consistent use over several months.

effects.label: Variable Response:
effects.description: Effectiveness appears highly dependent on the quality of the extract and individual biochemistry.

dosage.label: Prepared Root Powder:
dosage.description: Commonly taken in doses of 500mg to 1000mg daily in capsule or tea form.

dosage.label: Standardized Extracts:
dosage.description: 10:1 or 20:1 concentrated extracts are used at lower volumes, typically following manufacturer guidelines.

dosage.label: Traditional Decoction:
dosage.description: Slices of the prepared root are simmered for 20-30 minutes to create a medicinal tea.

sideEffects.label: Hepatotoxicity:
sideEffects.description: A major concern; excessive or improper use has been linked to drug-induced liver injury.

sideEffects.label: Digestive Upset:
sideEffects.description: Raw versions can act as a potent laxative and cause abdominal cramping.

sideEffects.label: Estrogenic Potential:
sideEffects.description: May have weak estrogen-like effects, requiring caution for those with hormone-sensitive conditions.

availability.label: Sourcing Quality:
availability.description: Crucial to buy from reputable suppliers who provide heavy metal and authenticity testing.

availability.label: Form Availability:
availability.description: Widely available as prepared (Sheng Shou Wu) or raw (He Shou Wu) powders, slices, and tinctures.
